11_6_16.NKJV: Then he built the twenty-cubit room at the rear of the temple, from floor to ceiling, with cedar boards; he built it inside as the inner sanctuary, as the Most Holy Place.
11_6_16.KJV: And he built twenty cubits on the sides of the house, both the floor and the walls with boards of cedar: he even built them for it within, even for the oracle, even for the most holy place.
11_6_16.ESV: He built twenty cubits of the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the walls, and he built this within as an inner sanctuary, as the Most Holy Place.
11_6_16.NASB: He built twenty cubits on the rear part of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the ceiling; he built [them] for it on the inside as an inner sanctuary, [even] as the most holy place.
11_6_16.NLT: He partitioned off an inner sanctuary — the Most Holy Place — at the far end of the Temple. It was 30 feet deep and was paneled with cedar from floor to ceiling.
11_6_16.WEB: He built twenty cubits on the back part of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the ceiling. He built them for it within, for an inner sanctuary, even for the most holy place.
11_6_16.CSB17: Then he lined thirty feet of the rear of the temple with cedar boards from the floor to the surface of the ceiling, and he built the interior as an inner sanctuary, the most holy place.
11_6_16.AMP: He built twenty cubits on the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the ceiling; he built its interior as the [inner] sanctuary, the Holy of Holies.
11_6_16.MSG: The thirty feet at the rear of The Temple he made into an Inner Sanctuary, cedar planks from floor to ceiling—the Holy of Holies.
11_6_16.NIV: He partitioned off twenty cubits at the rear of the temple with cedar boards from floor to ceiling to form within the temple an inner sanctuary, the Most Holy Place.
11_6_16.NRSVCE: He built twenty cubits of the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the rafters, and he built this within as an inner sanctuary, as the most holy place.

11_6_17.NKJV: And in front of it the temple sanctuary was forty cubits long.
11_6_17.KJV: And the house, that is, the temple before it, was forty cubits long.
11_6_17.ESV: The house, that is, the nave in front of the inner sanctuary, was forty cubits long.
11_6_17.NASB: The house, that is, the nave in front of [the inner sanctuary], was forty cubits [long].
11_6_17.NLT: The main room of the Temple, outside the Most Holy Place, was 60 feet long.
11_6_17.WEB: In front of the temple sanctuary was forty cubits.
11_6_17.CSB17: The temple, that is, the sanctuary in front of the most holy place, was sixty feet long.
11_6_17.AMP: The [rest of the] house, that is, the temple in front of the Holy of Holies, was forty cubits long.
11_6_17.MSG: The Main Sanctuary area in front was sixty feet long.
11_6_17.NIV: The main hall in front of this room was forty cubits long.
11_6_17.NRSVCE: The house, that is, the nave in front of the inner sanctuary, was forty cubits long.

11_6_18.NKJV: The inside of the temple was cedar, carved with ornamental buds and open flowers. All was cedar; there was no stone to be seen.
11_6_18.KJV: And the cedar of the house within was carved with knops and open flowers: all was cedar; there was no stone seen. knops: or, gourds open: Heb. openings of
11_6_18.ESV: The cedar within the house was carved in the form of gourds and open flowers. All was cedar; no stone was seen.
11_6_18.NASB: There was cedar on the house within, carved [in the shape] of gourds and open flowers; all was cedar, there was no stone seen.
11_6_18.NLT: Cedar paneling completely covered the stone walls throughout the Temple, and the paneling was decorated with carvings of gourds and open flowers.
11_6_18.WEB: There was cedar on the house within, carved with buds and open flowers. All was cedar. No stone was visible.
11_6_18.CSB17: The cedar paneling inside the temple was carved with ornamental gourds and flower blossoms. Everything was cedar; not a stone could be seen.
11_6_18.AMP: The cedar on the house within had wood carvings in the shape of gourds and open flowers. Everything was cedar; no stone was visible.
11_6_18.MSG: The entire interior of The Temple was cedar, with carvings of fruits and flowers. All cedar—none of the stone was exposed.
11_6_18.NIV: The inside of the temple was cedar, carved with gourds and open flowers. Everything was cedar; no stone was to be seen.
11_6_18.NRSVCE: The cedar within the house had carvings of gourds and open flowers; all was cedar, no stone was seen.

11_6_19.NKJV: And he prepared the inner sanctuary inside the temple, to set the ark of the covenant of the LORD there.
11_6_19.KJV: And the oracle he prepared in the house within, to set there the ark of the covenant of the LORD.
11_6_19.ESV: The inner sanctuary he prepared in the innermost part of the house, to set there the ark of the covenant of the LORD.
11_6_19.NASB: Then he prepared an inner sanctuary within the house in order to place there the ark of the covenant of the LORD.
11_6_19.NLT: He prepared the inner sanctuary at the far end of the Temple, where the Ark of the LORD’s Covenant would be placed.
11_6_19.WEB: He prepared an inner sanctuary in the middle of the house within, to set the ark of Yahweh’s covenant there.
11_6_19.CSB17: He prepared the inner sanctuary inside the temple to put the ark of the LORD’s covenant there.
11_6_19.AMP: Then he prepared the Holy of Holies within the house in order to put the ark of the covenant of the LORD there.
11_6_19.MSG: The Inner Sanctuary within The Temple was for housing the Chest of the Covenant of God.
11_6_19.NIV: He prepared the inner sanctuary within the temple to set the ark of the covenant of the Lord there.
11_6_19.NRSVCE: The inner sanctuary he prepared in the innermost part of the house, to set there the ark of the covenant of the LORD.

11_6_20.NKJV: The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and twenty cubits high. He overlaid it with pure gold, and overlaid the altar of cedar.
11_6_20.KJV: And the oracle in the forepart was twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits in breadth, and twenty cubits in the height thereof: and he overlaid it with pure gold; and so covered the altar which was of cedar. pure: Heb. shut up
11_6_20.ESV: The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and twenty cubits high, and he overlaid it with pure gold. He also overlaid an altar of cedar.
11_6_20.NASB: The inner sanctuary [was] twenty cubits in length, twenty cubits in width, and twenty cubits in height, and he overlaid it with pure gold. He also overlaid the altar with cedar.
11_6_20.NLT: This inner sanctuary was 30 feet long, 30 feet wide, and 30 feet high. He overlaid the inside with solid gold. He also overlaid the altar made of cedar.
11_6_20.WEB: Within the inner sanctuary was twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits in width, and twenty cubits in its height; and he overlaid it with pure gold; and he covered the altar with cedar.

11_6_20.AMP: The Holy of Holies was twenty cubits in length, twenty cubits in width, and twenty cubits in height (a cube), and he overlaid it with pure gold. He also overlaid the cedar altar [with gold].
11_6_20.MSG: This Inner Sanctuary was a cube, thirty feet each way, all plated with gold. The Altar of cedar was also gold-plated.
11_6_20.NIV: The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long, twenty wide and twenty high. He overlaid the inside with pure gold, and he also overlaid the altar of cedar.
11_6_20.NRSVCE: The interior of the inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and twenty cubits high; he overlaid it with pure gold. He also overlaid the altar with cedar.

11_6_21.NKJV: So Solomon overlaid the inside of the temple with pure gold. He stretched gold chains across the front of the inner sanctuary, and overlaid it with gold.
11_6_21.KJV: So Solomon overlaid the house within with pure gold: and he made a partition by the chains of gold before the oracle; and he overlaid it with gold.

11_6_21.NASB: So Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold. And he drew chains of gold across the front of the inner sanctuary, and he overlaid it with gold.
11_6_21.NLT: Then Solomon overlaid the rest of the Temple’s interior with solid gold, and he made gold chains to protect the entrance to the Most Holy Place.
11_6_21.WEB: So Solomon overlaid the house within with pure gold. He drew chains of gold across before the inner sanctuary, and he overlaid it with gold.
11_6_21.CSB17: Next, Solomon overlaid the interior of the temple with pure gold, and he hung gold chains across the front of the inner sanctuary and overlaid it with gold.
11_6_21.AMP: Solomon overlaid the interior of the house with pure gold, and he drew chains of gold across the front of the Holy of Holies (inner sanctuary), and he overlaid it with gold.
11_6_21.MSG: Everywhere you looked there was pure gold: gold chains strung in front of the gold-plated Inner Sanctuary
11_6_21.NIV: Solomon covered the inside of the temple with pure gold, and he extended gold chains across the front of the inner sanctuary, which was overlaid with gold.
11_6_21.NRSVCE: Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold, then he drew chains of gold across, in front of the inner sanctuary, and overlaid it with gold.

11_6_22.NKJV: The whole temple he overlaid with gold, until he had finished all the temple; also he overlaid with gold the entire altar that was by the inner sanctuary.
11_6_22.KJV: And the whole house he overlaid with gold, until he had finished all the house: also the whole altar that was by the oracle he overlaid with gold.
11_6_22.ESV: And he overlaid the whole house with gold, until all the house was finished. Also the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary he overlaid with gold.
11_6_22.NASB: He overlaid the whole house with gold, until all the house was finished. Also the whole altar which was by the inner sanctuary he overlaid with gold.
11_6_22.NLT: So he finished overlaying the entire Temple with gold, including the altar that belonged to the Most Holy Place.
11_6_22.WEB: He overlaid the whole house with gold, until all the house was finished. He also overlaid the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary with gold.
11_6_22.CSB17: So he added the gold overlay to the entire temple until everything was completely finished, including the entire altar that belongs to the inner sanctuary.
11_6_22.AMP: Then he overlaid the entire house with gold, until the whole house was finished. He also overlaid the entire [incense] altar which was by the Holy of Holies with gold.
11_6_22.MSG: —gold everywhere—walls, ceiling, floor, and Altar. Dazzling!
11_6_22.NIV: So he overlaid the whole interior with gold. He also overlaid with gold the altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary.
11_6_22.NRSVCE: Next he overlaid the whole house with gold, in order that the whole house might be perfect; even the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary he overlaid with gold.

11_6_23.NKJV: Inside the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olive wood, each ten cubits high.
11_6_23.KJV: And within the oracle he made two cherubims of olive tree, each ten cubits high. olive: or, oily: Heb. trees of oil
11_6_23.ESV: In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olivewood, each ten cubits high.
11_6_23.NASB: Also in the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olive wood, each ten cubits high.

11_6_23.NIV: In the inner sanctuary he made a pair of cherubim of olive wood, each ten cubits high.
11_6_23.NRSVCE: In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olivewood, each ten cubits high.

11_6_24.NKJV: One wing of the cherub was five cubits, and the other wing of the cherub five cubits: ten cubits from the tip of one wing to the tip of the other.
11_6_24.KJV: And five cubits was the one wing of the cherub, and five cubits the other wing of the cherub: from the uttermost part of the one wing unto the uttermost part of the other were ten cubits.
11_6_24.ESV: Five cubits was the length of one wing of the cherub, and five cubits the length of the other wing of the cherub; it was ten cubits from the tip of one wing to the tip of the other.
11_6_24.NASB: Five cubits [was] the one wing of the cherub and five cubits the other wing of the cherub; from the end of one wing to the end of the other wing [were] ten cubits.
11_6_24.NLT: The wingspan of each of the cherubim was 15 feet, each wing being 7 1⁄2 feet long.
11_6_24.WEB: Five cubits was the one wing of the cherub, and five cubits the other wing of the cherub. From the tip of one wing to the tip of the other was ten cubits.

The combined wingspread stretched the width of the room, the wing of one cherub touched one wall, the wing of the other the other wall, and the wings touched in the middle.
11_6_24.NIV: One wing of the first cherub was five cubits long, and the other wing five cubits—ten cubits from wing tip to wing tip.
11_6_24.NRSVCE: Five cubits was the length of one wing of the cherub, and five cubits the length of the other wing of the cherub; it was ten cubits from the tip of one wing to the tip of the other.

11_6_25.NASB: The other cherub [was] ten cubits; both the cherubim were of the same measure and the same form.

11_6_25.WEB: The other cherub was ten cubits. Both the cherubim were of one measure and one form.

11_6_25.AMP: The [wingspan of the] other cherub was also ten cubits. The measurements and cut (shape) of the two cherubim were the same;
11_6_25.NIV: The second cherub also measured ten cubits, for the two cherubim were identical in size and shape.
11_6_25.NRSVCE: The other cherub also measured ten cubits; both cherubim had the same measure and the same form.

11_6_26.NKJV: The height of one cherub was ten cubits, and so was the other cherub.
11_6_26.KJV: The height of the one cherub was ten cubits, and so was it of the other cherub.
11_6_26.ESV: The height of one cherub was ten cubits, and so was that of the other cherub.
11_6_26.NASB: The height of the one cherub [was] ten cubits, and so [was] the other cherub.
11_6_26.WEB: One cherub was ten cubits high, and so was the other cherub.
11_6_26.CSB17: The first cherub’s height was 15 feet and so was the second cherub’s.
11_6_26.AMP: the height of the one cherub was ten cubits, as was the other.

11_6_26.NRSVCE: The height of one cherub was ten cubits, and so was that of the other cherub.

11_6_27.NKJV: Then he set the cherubim inside the inner room; and they stretched out the wings of the cherubim so that the wing of the one touched one wall, and the wing of the other cherub touched the other wall. And their wings touched each other in the middle of the room.
11_6_27.KJV: And he set the cherubims within the inner house: and they stretched forth the wings of the cherubims, so that the wing of the one touched the one wall, and the wing of the other cherub touched the other wall; and their wings touched one another in the midst of the house. they: or, the cherubims stretched forth their wings
11_6_27.ESV: He put the cherubim in the innermost part of the house. And the wings of the cherubim were spread out so that a wing of one touched the one wall, and a wing of the other cherub touched the other wall; their other wings touched each other in the middle of the house.
11_6_27.NASB: He placed the cherubim in the midst of the inner house, and the wings of the cherubim were spread out, so that the wing of the one was touching the [one] wall, and the wing of the other cherub was touching the other wall. So their wings were touching each other in the center of the house.
11_6_27.NLT: He placed them side by side in the inner sanctuary of the Temple. Their outspread wings reached from wall to wall, while their inner wings touched at the center of the room.
11_6_27.WEB: He set the cherubim within the inner house. The wings of the cherubim were stretched out, so that the wing of the one touched the one wall, and the wing of the other cherub touched the other wall; and their wings touched one another in the middle of the house.
11_6_27.CSB17: Then he put the cherubim inside the inner temple. Since their wings were spread out, the first one’s wing touched one wall while the second cherub’s wing touched the other wall, and in the middle of the temple their wings were touching wing to wing.
11_6_27.AMP: He put the cherubim [above the ark] inside the innermost room of the house, and their wings were spread out so that the wing of the one touched one wall, and the wing of the other cherub was touching the other wall; and their inner wings were touching each other in the middle of the house.
11_6_27.NIV: He placed the cherubim inside the innermost room of the temple, with their wings spread out. The wing of one cherub touched one wall, while the wing of the other touched the other wall, and their wings touched each other in the middle of the room.
11_6_27.NRSVCE: He put the cherubim in the innermost part of the house; the wings of the cherubim were spread out so that a wing of one was touching the one wall, and a wing of the other cherub was touching the other wall; their other wings toward the center of the house were touching wing to wing.

11_6_28.NKJV: Also he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
11_6_28.KJV: And he overlaid the cherubims with gold.
11_6_28.ESV: And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
11_6_28.NASB: He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
11_6_28.NLT: He overlaid the two cherubim with gold.
11_6_28.WEB: He overlaid the cherubim with gold.
11_6_28.CSB17: He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
11_6_28.AMP: Solomon also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
11_6_28.NIV: He overlaid the cherubim with gold.
11_6_28.NRSVCE: He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.

11_6_29.NKJV: Then he carved all the walls of the temple all around, both the inner and outer sanctuaries, with carved figures of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers.
11_6_29.KJV: And he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved figures of cherubims and palm trees and open flowers, within and without. open flowers: Heb. openings of flowers
11_6_29.ESV: Around all the walls of the house he carved engraved figures of cherubim and palm trees and open flowers, in the inner and outer rooms.
11_6_29.NASB: Then he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved engravings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, inner and outer [sanctuaries].
11_6_29.NLT: He decorated all the walls of the inner sanctuary and the main room with carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers.
11_6_29.WEB: He carved all the walls of the house around with carved figures of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, inside and outside.
11_6_29.CSB17: He carved all the surrounding temple walls with carved engravings — cherubim, palm trees, and flower blossoms — in the inner and outer sanctuaries.
11_6_29.AMP: He carved all the walls of the house all around with carved engravings of cherubim, palm-shaped decorations, and open flowers, [both] the inner and the outer sanctuaries.
11_6_29.MSG: He then carved engravings of cherubim, palm trees, and flower blossoms on all the walls of both the Inner and the Main Sanctuary.
11_6_29.NIV: On the walls all around the temple, in both the inner and outer rooms, he carved cherubim, palm trees and open flowers.
11_6_29.NRSVCE: He carved the walls of the house all around about with carved engravings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, in the inner and outer rooms.

11_6_30.NKJV: And the floor of the temple he overlaid with gold, both the inner and outer sanctuaries.
11_6_30.KJV: And the floor of the house he overlaid with gold, within and without.
11_6_30.ESV: The floor of the house he overlaid with gold in the inner and outer rooms.
11_6_30.NASB: He overlaid the floor of the house with gold, inner and outer [sanctuaries].
11_6_30.NLT: He overlaid the floor in both rooms with gold.
11_6_30.WEB: He overlaid the floor of the house with gold, inside and outside.
11_6_30.CSB17: He overlaid the temple floor with gold in both the inner and the outer sanctuaries.
11_6_30.AMP: He overlaid the floor of the house with gold, [both] the inner and outer sanctuaries.
11_6_30.MSG: And all the floors of both inner and outer rooms were gold plated.
11_6_30.NIV: He also covered the floors of both the inner and outer rooms of the temple with gold.
11_6_30.NRSVCE: The floor of the house he overlaid with gold, in the inner and outer rooms.

11_6_31.NKJV: For the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood; the lintel and doorposts were one-fifth of the wall.
11_6_31.KJV: And for the entering of the oracle he made doors of olive tree: the lintel and side posts were a fifth part of the wall. a fifth: or, fivesquare
11_6_31.ESV: For the entrance to the inner sanctuary he made doors of olivewood; the lintel and the doorposts were five-sided.
11_6_31.NASB: For the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood, the lintel [and] five-sided doorposts.
11_6_31.NLT: For the entrance to the inner sanctuary, he made double doors of wild olive wood with five-sided doorposts.
11_6_31.WEB: For the entrance of the inner sanctuary, he made doors of olive wood. The lintel and door posts were a fifth part of the wall.
11_6_31.CSB17: For the entrance of the inner sanctuary, he made olive wood doors. The pillars of the doorposts were five-sided.
11_6_31.AMP: For the entrance of the Holy of Holies he made two [folding] doors of olive wood, the lintel (header above the door) and five-sided doorposts (frames).
11_6_31.MSG: He constructed doors of olivewood for the entrance to the Inner Sanctuary; the lintel and doorposts were five-sided.
11_6_31.NIV: For the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood with five-sided jambs.
11_6_31.NRSVCE: For the entrance to the inner sanctuary he made doors of olivewood; the lintel and the doorposts were five-sided.

11_6_32.NKJV: The two doors were of olive wood; and he carved on them figures of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold; and he spread gold on the cherubim and on the palm trees.
11_6_32.KJV: The two doors also were of olive tree; and he carved upon them carvings of cherubims and palm trees and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold, and spread gold upon the cherubims, and upon the palm trees. two: or, leaves of the doors open flowers: Heb. openings of flowers
11_6_32.ESV: He covered the two doors of olivewood with carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers. He overlaid them with gold and spread gold on the cherubim and on the palm trees.
11_6_32.NASB: So [he made] two doors of olive wood, and he carved on them carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold; and he spread the gold on the cherubim and on the palm trees.
11_6_32.NLT: These double doors were decorated with carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers. The doors, including the decorations of cherubim and palm trees, were overlaid with gold.
11_6_32.WEB: So he made two doors of olive wood; and he carved on them carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold. He spread the gold on the cherubim and on the palm trees.
11_6_32.CSB17: The two doors were made of olive wood. He carved cherubim, palm trees, and flower blossoms on them and overlaid them with gold, hammering gold over the cherubim and palm trees.
11_6_32.AMP: So he made two doors of olive wood, and he carved on them carvings of cherubim, palm-shaped decorations, and open flowers; and overlaid them with gold; and he hammered out overlays of gold on the cherubim and palm decorations.
11_6_32.MSG: The doors were also carved with cherubim, palm trees, and flowers, and then covered with gold leaf.
11_6_32.NIV: And on the two olive wood doors he carved cherubim, palm trees and open flowers, and overlaid the cherubim and palm trees with beaten gold.
11_6_32.NRSVCE: He covered the two doors of olivewood with carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers; he overlaid them with gold, and spread gold on the cherubim and on the palm trees.

11_6_33.NKJV: So for the door of the sanctuary he also made doorposts of olive wood, one-fourth of the wall.
11_6_33.KJV: So also made he for the door of the temple posts of olive tree, a fourth part of the wall. a fourth: or, foursquare
11_6_33.ESV: So also he made for the entrance to the nave doorposts of olivewood, in the form of a square,
11_6_33.NASB: So also he made for the entrance of the nave four-sided doorposts of olive wood
11_6_33.NLT: Then he made four-sided doorposts of wild olive wood for the entrance to the Temple.
11_6_33.WEB: He also did so for the entrance of the temple door posts of olive wood, out of a fourth part of the wall;
11_6_33.CSB17: In the same way, he made four-sided olive wood doorposts for the sanctuary entrance.
11_6_33.AMP: Also he made for the entrance of the [outer] sanctuary (the Holy Place) four-sided doorposts (frames) of olive wood
11_6_33.MSG: Similarly, he built the entrance to the Main Sanctuary using olivewood for the doorposts but these doorposts were four-sided.
11_6_33.NIV: In the same way he made four-sided jambs of olive wood for the entrance to the main hall.
11_6_33.NRSVCE: So also he made for the entrance to the nave doorposts of olivewood, four-sided each,

11_6_34.NKJV: And the two doors were of cypress wood; two panels comprised one folding door, and two panels comprised the other folding door.
11_6_34.KJV: And the two doors were of fir tree: the two leaves of the one door were folding, and the two leaves of the other door were folding.
11_6_34.ESV: and two doors of cypress wood. The two leaves of the one door were folding, and the two leaves of the other door were folding.
11_6_34.NASB: and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door turned on pivots, and the two leaves of the other door turned on pivots.
11_6_34.NLT: There were two folding doors of cypress wood, and each door was hinged to fold back upon itself.
11_6_34.WEB: and two doors of cypress wood. The two leaves of the one door were folding, and the two leaves of the other door were folding.
11_6_34.CSB17: The two doors were made of cypress wood; the first door had two folding sides, and the second door had two folding panels.
11_6_34.AMP: and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door turned on pivots and were folding, and the two leaves of the other door also turned on pivots.
11_6_34.MSG: The doors were of cypress, split into two panels, each panel swinging separately.
11_6_34.NIV: He also made two pine doors, each having two leaves that turned in sockets.
11_6_34.NRSVCE: and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door were folding, and the two leaves of the other door were folding.

11_6_35.NKJV: Then he carved cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers on them, and overlaid them with gold applied evenly on the carved work.
11_6_35.KJV: And he carved thereon cherubims and palm trees and open flowers: and covered them with gold fitted upon the carved work.
11_6_35.ESV: On them he carved cherubim and palm trees and open flowers, and he overlaid them with gold evenly applied on the carved work.
11_6_35.NASB: He carved [on it] cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers; and he overlaid [them] with gold evenly applied on the engraved work.
11_6_35.NLT: These doors were decorated with carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers — all overlaid evenly with gold.
11_6_35.WEB: He carved cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers; and he overlaid them with gold fitted on the engraved work.
11_6_35.CSB17: He carved cherubim, palm trees, and flower blossoms on them and overlaid them with gold applied evenly over the carving.
11_6_35.AMP: He carved cherubim, palm-shaped decorations, and open flowers on the doors, and overlaid them with gold evenly applied on the carved work.
11_6_35.MSG: These also were carved with cherubim, palm trees, and flowers, and plated with finely hammered gold leaf.
11_6_35.NIV: He carved cherubim, palm trees and open flowers on them and overlaid them with gold hammered evenly over the carvings.
11_6_35.NRSVCE: He carved cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, overlaying them with gold evenly applied upon the carved work.

11_6_36.NKJV: And he built the inner court with three rows of hewn stone and a row of cedar beams.
11_6_36.KJV: And he built the inner court with three rows of hewed stone, and a row of cedar beams.
11_6_36.ESV: He built the inner court with three courses of cut stone and one course of cedar beams.
11_6_36.NASB: He built the inner court with three rows of cut stone and a row of cedar beams.
11_6_36.NLT: The walls of the inner courtyard were built so that there was one layer of cedar beams between every three layers of finished stone.
11_6_36.WEB: He built the inner court with three courses of cut stone and a course of cedar beams.
11_6_36.CSB17: He built the inner courtyard with three rows of dressed stone and a row of trimmed cedar beams.
11_6_36.AMP: He built the inner courtyard with three rows of cut stone and a row of cedar beams.
11_6_36.MSG: He built the inner court with three courses of dressed stones topped with a course of planed cedar timbers.
11_6_36.NIV: And he built the inner courtyard of three courses of dressed stone and one course of trimmed cedar beams.
11_6_36.NRSVCE: He built the inner court with three courses of dressed stone to one course of cedar beams.

11_6_37.NKJV: In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid, in the month of Ziv.
11_6_37.KJV: In the fourth year was the foundation of the house of the LORD laid, in the month Zif:
11_6_37.ESV: In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid, in the month of Ziv.
11_6_37.NASB: In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid, in the month of Ziv.
11_6_37.NLT: The foundation of the LORD’s Temple was laid in midspring, in the month of Ziv, during the fourth year of Solomon’s reign.
11_6_37.WEB: The foundation of Yahweh’s house was laid in the fourth year, in the month Ziv.
11_6_37.CSB17: The foundation of the LORD’s temple was laid in Solomon’s fourth year in the month of Ziv.
11_6_37.AMP: In the fourth year [of King Solomon's reign] the foundation of the Lord's house was laid, in the [second] month, Ziv (April-May).
11_6_37.MSG: The foundation for God's Temple was laid in the fourth year in the month of Ziv.
11_6_37.NIV: The foundation of the temple of the Lord was laid in the fourth year, in the month of Ziv.
11_6_37.NRSVCE: In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid, in the month of Ziv.

11_6_38.NKJV: And in the eleventh year, in the month of Bul, which is the eighth month, the house was finished in all its details and according to all its plans. So he was seven years in building it.
11_6_38.KJV: And in the eleventh year, in the month Bul, which is the eighth month, was the house finished throughout all the parts thereof, and according to all the fashion of it. So was he seven years in building it. through out: or, with all the parts thereof, and with all the ordinances thereof
11_6_38.ESV: And in the eleventh year, in the month of Bul, which is the eighth month, the house was finished in all its parts, and according to all its specifications. He was seven years in building it.
11_6_38.NASB: In the eleventh year, in the month of Bul, which is the eighth month, the house was finished throughout all its parts and according to all its plans. So he was seven years in building it.
11_6_38.NLT: The entire building was completed in every detail by midautumn, in the month of Bul, during the eleventh year of his reign. So it took seven years to build the Temple.
11_6_38.WEB: In the eleventh year, in the month Bul, which is the eighth month, the house was finished throughout all its parts, and according to all its specifications. So he spent seven years building it.
11_6_38.CSB17: In his eleventh year in the month of Bul, which is the eighth month, the temple was completed in every detail and according to every specification. So he built it in seven years.
11_6_38.AMP: In the eleventh year [of King Solomon's reign] in the month of Bul (October-November), that is, the eighth month, the house was finished throughout all its parts and in accordance with all its specifications. So he built it in seven years.
11_6_38.MSG: It was completed in the eleventh year in the month of Bul (the eighth month) down to the last detail, just as planned. It took Solomon seven years to build it.
11_6_38.NIV: In the eleventh year in the month of Bul, the eighth month, the temple was finished in all its details according to its specifications. He had spent seven years building it.
11_6_38.NRSVCE: In the eleventh year, in the month of Bul, which is the eighth month, the house was finished in all its parts, and according to all its specifications. He was seven years in building it.

11_7_1.NKJV: But Solomon took thirteen years to build his own house; so he finished all his house.
11_7_1.KJV: But Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished all his house.
11_7_1.ESV: Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished his entire house.
11_7_1.NASB: Now Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished all his house.
11_7_1.NLT: Solomon also built a palace for himself, and it took him thirteen years to complete the construction.
11_7_1.WEB: Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished all his house.
11_7_1.CSB17: Solomon completed his entire palace complex after thirteen years of construction.
11_7_1.AMP: Now Solomon built his own house (palace) in thirteen years, and he finished all of his house [in that time].
11_7_1.MSG: It took Solomon another thirteen years to finish building his own palace complex.
11_7_1.NIV: Solomon Builds His PalaceIt took Solomon thirteen years, however, to complete the construction of his palace.
11_7_1.NRSVCE: Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished his entire house.

11_7_2.NKJV: He also built the House of the Forest of Lebanon; its length was one hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits, with four rows of cedar pillars, and cedar beams on the pillars.
11_7_2.KJV: He built also the house of the forest of Lebanon; the length thereof was an hundred cubits, and the breadth thereof fifty cubits, and the height thereof thirty cubits, upon four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams upon the pillars.
11_7_2.ESV: He built the House of the Forest of Lebanon. Its length was a hundred cubits and its breadth fifty cubits and its height thirty cubits, and it was built on four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams on the pillars.
11_7_2.NASB: He built the house of the forest of Lebanon; its length was 100 cubits and its width 50 cubits and its height 30 cubits, on four rows of cedar pillars with cedar beams on the pillars.
11_7_2.NLT: One of Solomon’s buildings was called the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon. It was 150 feet long, 75 feet wide, and 45 feet high. There were four rows of cedar pillars, and great cedar beams rested on the pillars.
11_7_2.WEB: For he built the House of the Forest of Lebanon. Its length was one hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits, on four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams on the pillars.
11_7_2.CSB17: He built the House of the Forest of Lebanon. It was one hundred fifty feet long, seventy-five feet wide, and forty-five feet high on four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams on top of the pillars.
11_7_2.AMP: He also built the House of the Forest of Lebanon; its length was a hundred cubits (150 ft.), its width fifty cubits (75 ft.), and its height thirty cubits (45 ft.), upon four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams upon the pillars.
11_7_2.MSG: He built the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon a hundred and fifty feet long, seventy-five feet wide, and forty-five feet high.
11_7_2.NIV: He built the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon a hundred cubits long, fifty wide and thirty high, with four rows of cedar columns supporting trimmed cedar beams.
11_7_2.NRSVCE: He built the House of the Forest of the Lebanon one hundred cubits long, fifty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high, built on four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams on the pillars.

11_7_3.NKJV: And it was paneled with cedar above the beams that were on forty-five pillars, fifteen to a row.
11_7_3.KJV: And it was covered with cedar above upon the beams, that lay on forty five pillars, fifteen in a row. beams: Heb. ribs
11_7_3.ESV: And it was covered with cedar above the chambers that were on the forty-five pillars, fifteen in each row.
11_7_3.NASB: It was paneled with cedar above the side chambers which were on the 45 pillars, 15 in each row.
11_7_3.NLT: The hall had a cedar roof. Above the beams on the pillars were forty-five side rooms, arranged in three tiers of fifteen each.
11_7_3.WEB: It was covered with cedar above over the forty-five beams, that were on the pillars, fifteen in a row.
11_7_3.CSB17: It was paneled above with cedar at the top of the chambers that rested on forty-five pillars, fifteen per row.
11_7_3.AMP: And it was covered with cedar [as a roof] on the supporting beams that were upon the forty-five pillars, fifteen in each row.
11_7_3.MSG: There were four rows of cedar columns supporting forty-five cedar beams, fifteen in each row, and then roofed with cedar.
11_7_3.NIV: It was roofed with cedar above the beams that rested on the columns—forty-five beams, fifteen to a row.
11_7_3.NRSVCE: It was roofed with cedar on the forty-five rafters, fifteen in each row, which were on the pillars.

11_7_4.NKJV: There were windows with beveled frames in three rows, and window was opposite window in three tiers.
11_7_4.KJV: And there were windows in three rows, and light was against light in three ranks. light was: Heb. sight against sight
11_7_4.ESV: There were window frames in three rows, and window opposite window in three tiers.
11_7_4.NASB: [There were artistic window] frames in three rows, and window was opposite window in three ranks.
11_7_4.NLT: On each end of the long hall were three rows of windows facing each other.
11_7_4.WEB: There were beams in three rows, and window was facing window in three ranks.
11_7_4.CSB17: There were three rows of window frames, facing each other in three tiers.
11_7_4.AMP: There were artistic window frames in three rows, and window was opposite window in three tiers.
11_7_4.MSG: Windows in groupings of three were set high in the walls on either side.
11_7_4.NIV: Its windows were placed high in sets of three, facing each other.
11_7_4.NRSVCE: There were window frames in the three rows, facing each other in the three rows.

11_7_5.NKJV: And all the doorways and doorposts had rectangular frames; and window was opposite window in three tiers.
11_7_5.KJV: And all the doors and posts were square, with the windows: and light was against light in three ranks. doors: or, spaces and pillars were square in prospect
11_7_5.ESV: All the doorways and windows had square frames, and window was opposite window in three tiers.
11_7_5.NASB: All the doorways and doorposts [had] squared [artistic] frames, and window was opposite window in three ranks.
11_7_5.NLT: All the doorways and doorposts had rectangular frames and were arranged in sets of three, facing each other.
11_7_5.WEB: All the doors and posts were made square with beams: and window was facing window in three ranks.
11_7_5.CSB17: All the doors and doorposts had rectangular frames, the openings facing each other in three tiers.
11_7_5.AMP: All the doorways and doorposts [and windows] had squared [artistic] frames, and window was opposite window in three tiers.
11_7_5.MSG: All the doors were rectangular and arranged symmetrically.
11_7_5.NIV: All the doorways had rectangular frames; they were in the front part in sets of three, facing each other.
11_7_5.NRSVCE: All the doorways and doorposts had four-sided frames, opposite, facing each other in the three rows.

11_7_6.NKJV: He also made the Hall of Pillars: its length was fifty cubits, and its width thirty cubits; and in front of them was a portico with pillars, and a canopy was in front of them.
11_7_6.KJV: And he made a porch of pillars; the length thereof was fifty cubits, and the breadth thereof thirty cubits: and the porch was before them: and the other pillars and the thick beam were before them. before them: or, according to them
11_7_6.ESV: And he made the Hall of Pillars; its length was fifty cubits, and its breadth thirty cubits. There was a porch in front with pillars, and a canopy in front of them.
11_7_6.NASB: Then he made the hall of pillars; its length was 50 cubits and its width 30 cubits, and a porch [was] in front of them and pillars and a threshold in front of them.
11_7_6.NLT: Solomon also built the Hall of Pillars, which was 75 feet long and 45 feet wide. There was a porch in front, along with a canopy supported by pillars.
11_7_6.WEB: He made the porch of pillars. Its length was fifty cubits and its width thirty cubits; with a porch before them, and pillars and a threshold before them.
11_7_6.CSB17: He made the hall of pillars seventy-five feet long and forty-five feet wide. A portico was in front of the pillars, and a canopy with pillars was in front of them.
11_7_6.AMP: He also made the Hall of Pillars; its length was fifty cubits (75 ft.) and its width thirty cubits (45 ft.). There was a porch in front, and pillars and a threshold in front of them.
11_7_6.MSG: He built a colonnaded courtyard seventy-five feet long and forty-five wide. It had a roofed porch at the front with ample eaves.
11_7_6.NIV: He made a colonnade fifty cubits long and thirty wide. In front of it was a portico, and in front of that were pillars and an overhanging roof.
11_7_6.NRSVCE: He made the Hall of Pillars fifty cubits long and thirty cubits wide. There was a porch in front with pillars, and a canopy in front of them.

11_7_7.NKJV: Then he made a hall for the throne, the Hall of Judgment, where he might judge; and it was paneled with cedar from floor to ceiling.
11_7_7.KJV: Then he made a porch for the throne where he might judge, even the porch of judgment: and it was covered with cedar from one side of the floor to the other. from: Heb. from floor to floor
11_7_7.ESV: And he made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment, even the Hall of Judgment. It was finished with cedar from floor to rafters.
11_7_7.NASB: He made the hall of the throne where he was to judge, the hall of judgment, and it was paneled with cedar from floor to floor.
11_7_7.NLT: Solomon also built the throne room, known as the Hall of Justice, where he sat to hear legal matters. It was paneled with cedar from floor to ceiling.
11_7_7.WEB: He made the porch of the throne where he was to judge, even the porch of judgment; and it was covered with cedar from floor to floor.
11_7_7.CSB17: He made the Hall of the Throne where he would judge — the Hall of Judgment. It was paneled with cedar from the floor to the rafters.
11_7_7.AMP: He made the hall for the throne where he was to judge, the Hall of Judgment; it was paneled with cedar from [one] floor to [another] floor.
11_7_7.MSG: He built a court room, the Hall of Justice, where he would decide judicial matters, and paneled it with cedar.
11_7_7.NIV: He built the throne hall, the Hall of Justice, where he was to judge, and he covered it with cedar from floor to ceiling.
11_7_7.NRSVCE: He made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment, the Hall of Justice, covered with cedar from floor to floor.

11_7_8.NKJV: And the house where he dwelt had another court inside the hall, of like workmanship. Solomon also made a house like this hall for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had taken as wife.
11_7_8.KJV: And his house where he dwelt had another court within the porch, which was of the like work. Solomon made also an house for Pharaoh's daughter, whom he had taken to wife, like unto this porch.
11_7_8.ESV: His own house where he was to dwell, in the other court back of the hall, was of like workmanship. Solomon also made a house like this hall for Pharaoh's daughter whom he had taken in marriage.
11_7_8.NASB: His house where he was to live, the other court inward from the hall, was of the same workmanship. He also made a house like this hall for Pharaoh's daughter, whom Solomon had married.
11_7_8.NLT: Solomon’s living quarters surrounded a courtyard behind this hall, and they were constructed the same way. He also built similar living quarters for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had married.
11_7_8.WEB: His house where he was to dwell, the other court within the porch, was of the like work. He made also a house for Pharaoh’s daughter (whom Solomon had taken as wife), like this porch.
11_7_8.CSB17: Solomon’s own palace where he would live, in the other courtyard behind the hall, was of similar construction. And he made a house like this hall for Pharaoh’s daughter, his wife.
11_7_8.AMP: His house where he was to live, the other courtyard behind the hall, was of similar workmanship. Solomon also made a house like this hall for Pharaoh's daughter, whom he had married.
11_7_8.MSG: He built his personal residence behind the Hall on a similar plan. Solomon also built another one just like it for Pharaoh's daughter, whom he had married.
11_7_8.NIV: And the palace in which he was to live, set farther back, was similar in design. Solomon also made a palace like this hall for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had married.
11_7_8.NRSVCE: His own house where he would reside, in the other court back of the hall, was of the same construction. Solomon also made a house like this hall for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had taken in marriage.

11_7_9.NKJV: All these were of costly stones cut to size, trimmed with saws, inside and out, from the foundation to the eaves, and also on the outside to the great court.
11_7_9.KJV: All these were of costly stones, according to the measures of hewed stones, sawed with saws, within and without, even from the foundation unto the coping, and so on the outside toward the great court.
11_7_9.ESV: All these were made of costly stones, cut according to measure, sawed with saws, back and front, even from the foundation to the coping, and from the outside to the great court.
11_7_9.NASB: All these were of costly stones, of stone cut according to measure, sawed with saws, inside and outside; even from the foundation to the coping, and so on the outside to the great court.
11_7_9.NLT: From foundation to eaves, all these buildings were built from huge blocks of high-quality stone, cut with saws and trimmed to exact measure on all sides.
11_7_9.WEB: All these were of costly stones, even of cut stone, according to measure, sawed with saws, inside and outside, even from the foundation to the coping, and so on the outside to the great court.
11_7_9.CSB17: All of these buildings were of costly stones, cut to size and sawed with saws on the inner and outer surfaces, from foundation to coping and from the outside to the great courtyard.
11_7_9.AMP: All these were of expensive stones, of stone cut according to measure, sawed with saws, inside and outside; even from the foundation to the coping, and from the outside to the great courtyard.
11_7_9.MSG: No expense was spared—everything here, inside and out, from foundation to roof was constructed using high-quality stone, accurately cut and shaped and polished.
11_7_9.NIV: All these structures, from the outside to the great courtyard and from foundation to eaves, were made of blocks of high-grade stone cut to size and trimmed with a saw on their inner and outer faces.
11_7_9.NRSVCE: All these were made of costly stones, cut according to measure, sawed with saws, back and front, from the foundation to the coping, and from outside to the great court.

11_7_10.NKJV: The foundation was of costly stones, large stones, some ten cubits and some eight cubits.
11_7_10.KJV: And the foundation was of costly stones, even great stones, stones of ten cubits, and stones of eight cubits.
11_7_10.ESV: The foundation was of costly stones, huge stones, stones of eight and ten cubits.
11_7_10.NASB: The foundation was of costly stones, [even] large stones, stones of ten cubits and stones of eight cubits.
11_7_10.NLT: Some of the huge foundation stones were 15 feet long, and some were 12 feet long.
11_7_10.WEB: The foundation was of costly stones, even great stones, stones of ten cubits, and stones of eight cubits.
11_7_10.CSB17: The foundation was made of large, costly stones twelve and fifteen feet long.
11_7_10.AMP: The foundation was of expensive stones, large stones, stones of ten cubits and stones of eight cubits.
11_7_10.MSG: The foundation stones were huge, ranging in size from twelve to fifteen feet, and of the very best quality.
11_7_10.NIV: The foundations were laid with large stones of good quality, some measuring ten cubits and some eight.
11_7_10.NRSVCE: The foundation was of costly stones, huge stones, stones of eight and ten cubits.

11_7_11.NKJV: And above were costly stones, hewn to size, and cedar wood.
11_7_11.KJV: And above were costly stones, after the measures of hewed stones, and cedars.
11_7_11.ESV: And above were costly stones, cut according to measurement, and cedar.
11_7_11.NASB: And above were costly stones, stone cut according to measure, and cedar.
11_7_11.NLT: The blocks of high-quality stone used in the walls were also cut to measure, and cedar beams were also used.
11_7_11.WEB: Above were costly stones, even cut stone, according to measure, and cedar wood.
11_7_11.CSB17: Above were also costly stones, cut to size, as well as cedar wood.
11_7_11.AMP: And above [the foundation] were expensive stones, stones cut according to measure, and cedar.
11_7_11.MSG: The finest stone was used above the foundation, shaped to size and trimmed with cedar.
11_7_11.NIV: Above were high-grade stones, cut to size, and cedar beams.
11_7_11.NRSVCE: There were costly stones above, cut to measure, and cedarwood.

11_7_12.NKJV: The great court was enclosed with three rows of hewn stones and a row of cedar beams. So were the inner court of the house of the LORD and the vestibule of the temple.
11_7_12.KJV: And the great court round about was with three rows of hewed stones, and a row of cedar beams, both for the inner court of the house of the LORD, and for the porch of the house.
11_7_12.ESV: The great court had three courses of cut stone all around, and a course of cedar beams; so had the inner court of the house of the LORD and the vestibule of the house.
11_7_12.NASB: So the great court all around [had] three rows of cut stone and a row of cedar beams even as the inner court of the house of the LORD, and the porch of the house.
11_7_12.NLT: The walls of the great courtyard were built so that there was one layer of cedar beams between every three layers of finished stone, just like the walls of the inner courtyard of the LORD’s Temple with its entry room.
11_7_12.WEB: The great court around had three courses of cut stone, and a course of cedar beams; like the inner court of Yahweh’s house and the porch of the house.
11_7_12.CSB17: Around the great courtyard, as well as the inner courtyard of the LORD’s temple and the portico of the temple, were three rows of dressed stone and a row of trimmed cedar beams.
11_7_12.AMP: So the great courtyard all around had three rows of cut stone and a row of cedar beams like the inner courtyard of the house of the LORD, and the porch of the house.
11_7_12.MSG: The courtyard was enclosed with a wall made of three layers of stone and topped with cedar timbers, just like the one in the porch of The Temple of God.
11_7_12.NIV: The great courtyard was surrounded by a wall of three courses of dressed stone and one course of trimmed cedar beams, as was the inner courtyard of the temple of the Lord with its portico.
11_7_12.NRSVCE: The great court had three courses of dressed stone to one layer of cedar beams all around; so had the inner court of the house of the LORD, and the vestibule of the house.

11_7_13.NKJV: Now King Solomon sent and brought Huram from Tyre.
11_7_13.KJV: And king Solomon sent and fetched Hiram out of Tyre.
11_7_13.ESV: And King Solomon sent and brought Hiram from Tyre.
11_7_13.NASB: Now King Solomon sent and brought Hiram from Tyre.
11_7_13.NLT: King Solomon then asked for a man named Huram to come from Tyre.
11_7_13.WEB: King Solomon sent and brought Hiram out of Tyre.
11_7_13.CSB17: King Solomon had Hiram brought from Tyre.
11_7_13.AMP: Now King Solomon sent word and brought Hiram [a skilled craftsman] from Tyre.
11_7_13.MSG: King Solomon sent to Tyre and asked Hiram (not the king; another Hiram) to come.
11_7_13.NIV: The Temple’s FurnishingsKing Solomon sent to Tyre and brought Huram,
11_7_13.NRSVCE: Now King Solomon invited and received Hiram from Tyre.

11_7_14.NKJV: He was the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a bronze worker; he was filled with wisdom and understanding and skill in working with all kinds of bronze work. So he came to King Solomon and did all his work.
11_7_14.KJV: He was a widow's son of the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in brass: and he was filled with wisdom, and understanding, and cunning to work all works in brass. And he came to king Solomon, and wrought all his work. a widow's: Heb. the son of a widow woman
11_7_14.ESV: He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in bronze. And he was full of wisdom, understanding, and skill for making any work in bronze. He came to King Solomon and did all his work.
11_7_14.NASB: He was a widow's son from the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in bronze; and he was filled with wisdom and understanding and skill for doing any work in bronze. So he came to King Solomon and performed all his work.
11_7_14.NLT: He was half Israelite, since his mother was a widow from the tribe of Naphtali, and his father had been a craftsman in bronze from Tyre. Huram was extremely skillful and talented in any work in bronze, and he came to do all the metal work for King Solomon.
11_7_14.WEB: He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in brass; and he was filled with wisdom and understanding and skill, to work all works in brass. He came to king Solomon, and performed all his work.
11_7_14.CSB17: He was a widow’s son from the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a bronze craftsman. Hiram had great skill, understanding, and knowledge to do every kind of bronze work. So he came to King Solomon and carried out all his work.
11_7_14.AMP: He was the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a craftsman in bronze. Hiram was filled with wisdom, understanding, and skill for doing any [kind of] work in bronze. So he came to King Solomon and did all his [bronze] work.
11_7_14.MSG: Hiram's mother was a widow from the tribe of Naphtali. His father was a Tyrian and a master worker in bronze. Hiram was a real artist—he could do anything with bronze. He came to King Solomon and did all the bronze work.
11_7_14.NIV: whose mother was a widow from the tribe of Naphtali and whose father was a man of Tyre and a craftsman in bronze. Huram was highly skilled and experienced in all kinds of bronze work. He came to King Solomon and did all the work assigned to him.
11_7_14.NRSVCE: He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali, whose father, a man of Tyre, had been an artisan in bronze; he was full of skill, intelligence, and knowledge in working bronze. He came to King Solomon, and did all his work.

11_7_15.NKJV: And he cast two pillars of bronze, each one eighteen cubits high, and a line of twelve cubits measured the circumference of each.
11_7_15.KJV: For he cast two pillars of brass, of eighteen cubits high apiece : and a line of twelve cubits did compass either of them about. cast: Heb. fashioned
11_7_15.ESV: He cast two pillars of bronze. Eighteen cubits was the height of one pillar, and a line of twelve cubits measured its circumference. It was hollow, and its thickness was four fingers. The second pillar was the same.
11_7_15.NASB: He fashioned the two pillars of bronze; eighteen cubits was the height of one pillar, and a line of twelve cubits measured the circumference of both.
11_7_15.NLT: Huram cast two bronze pillars, each 27 feet tall and 18 feet in circumference.
11_7_15.WEB: For he fashioned the two pillars of brass, eighteen cubits high apiece; and a line of twelve cubits encircled either of them.
11_7_15.CSB17: He cast two bronze pillars, each 27 feet high and 18 feet in circumference.
11_7_15.AMP: He cast the two pillars of bronze; the one pillar was eighteen cubits high, and a [measuring] line of twelve cubits measured the circumference of both.
11_7_15.MSG: First he cast two pillars in bronze, each twenty-seven feet tall and eighteen feet in circumference.
11_7_15.NIV: He cast two bronze pillars, each eighteen cubits high and twelve cubits around, by line.
11_7_15.NRSVCE: He cast two pillars of bronze. Eighteen cubits was the height of the one, and a cord of twelve cubits would encircle it; the second pillar was the same.

11_7_16.NKJV: Then he made two capitals of cast bronze, to set on the tops of the pillars. The height of one capital was five cubits, and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
11_7_16.KJV: And he made two chapiters of molten brass, to set upon the tops of the pillars: the height of the one chapiter was five cubits, and the height of the other chapiter was five cubits:
11_7_16.ESV: He also made two capitals of cast bronze to set on the tops of the pillars. The height of the one capital was five cubits, and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
11_7_16.NASB: He also made two capitals of molten bronze to set on the tops of the pillars; the height of the one capital was five cubits and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
11_7_16.NLT: For the tops of the pillars he cast bronze capitals, each 7 1⁄2 feet tall.
11_7_16.WEB: He made two capitals of molten brass, to set on the tops of the pillars. The height of the one capital was five cubits, and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
11_7_16.CSB17: He also made two capitals of cast bronze to set on top of the pillars; 7½ feet was the height of the first capital, and 7½ feet was also the height of the second capital.
11_7_16.AMP: He also made two capitals (crowns) of cast bronze to put on the tops of the pillars; the height of one capital was five cubits, and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
11_7_16.MSG: He then cast two capitals in bronze to set on the pillars; each capital was seven and a half feet high
11_7_16.NIV: He also made two capitals of cast bronze to set on the tops of the pillars; each capital was five cubits high.
11_7_16.NRSVCE: He also made two capitals of molten bronze, to set on the tops of the pillars; the height of the one capital was five cubits, and the height of the other capital was five cubits.

11_7_17.NKJV: He made a lattice network, with wreaths of chainwork, for the capitals which were on top of the pillars: seven chains for one capital and seven for the other capital.
11_7_17.KJV: And nets of checker work, and wreaths of chain work, for the chapiters which were upon the top of the pillars; seven for the one chapiter, and seven for the other chapiter.
11_7_17.ESV: There were lattices of checker work with wreaths of chain work for the capitals on the tops of the pillars, a lattice for the one capital and a lattice for the other capital.
11_7_17.NASB: [There were] nets of network and twisted threads of chainwork for the capitals which were on the top of the pillars; seven for the one capital and seven for the other capital.
11_7_17.NLT: Each capital was decorated with seven sets of latticework and interwoven chains.
11_7_17.WEB: There were nets of checker work, and wreaths of chain work, for the capitals which were on the top of the pillars; seven for the one capital, and seven for the other capital.
11_7_17.CSB17: The capitals on top of the pillars had gratings of latticework, wreaths made of chainwork — seven for the first capital and seven for the second.
11_7_17.AMP: There were nets of network (lattice-work) and twisted threads (wreaths) of chainwork for the capitals which were on the tops of the pillars, seven for one capital and seven for the other.
11_7_17.MSG: and flared at the top in the shape of a lily. Each capital was dressed with an elaborate filigree of seven braided chains and a double row of two hundred pomegranates, setting the pillars off magnificently.
11_7_17.NIV: A network of interwoven chains festooned the capitals on top of the pillars, seven for each capital.
11_7_17.NRSVCE: There were nets of checker work with wreaths of chain work for the capitals on the tops of the pillars; seven for the one capital, and seven for the other capital.

11_7_18.NKJV: So he made the pillars, and two rows of pomegranates above the network all around to cover the capitals that were on top; and thus he did for the other capital.
11_7_18.KJV: And he made the pillars, and two rows round about upon the one network, to cover the chapiters that were upon the top, with pomegranates: and so did he for the other chapiter.
11_7_18.ESV: Likewise he made pomegranates in two rows around the one latticework to cover the capital that was on the top of the pillar, and he did the same with the other capital.
11_7_18.NASB: So he made the pillars, and two rows around on the one network to cover the capitals which were on the top of the pomegranates; and so he did for the other capital.
11_7_18.NLT: He also encircled the latticework with two rows of pomegranates to decorate the capitals over the pillars.
11_7_18.WEB: So he made the pillars; and there were two rows around on the one network, to cover the capitals that were on the top of the pillars: and he did so for the other capital.
11_7_18.CSB17: He made the pillars with two encircling rows of pomegranates on the one grating to cover the capital on top; he did the same for the second capital.
11_7_18.AMP: So Hiram made the pillars [in this manner], and two rows around on the one network to cover the capitals which were on the top of the pomegranates; and he did the same for the other capital.
11_7_18.NIV: He made pomegranates in two rows encircling each network to decorate the capitals on top of the pillars. He did the same for each capital.
11_7_18.NRSVCE: He made the columns with two rows around each latticework to cover the capitals that were above the pomegranates; he did the same with the other capital.

11_7_19.NKJV: The capitals which were on top of the pillars in the hall were in the shape of lilies, four cubits.
11_7_19.KJV: And the chapiters that were upon the top of the pillars were of lily work in the porch, four cubits.
11_7_19.ESV: Now the capitals that were on the tops of the pillars in the vestibule were of lily-work, four cubits.
11_7_19.NASB: The capitals which [were] on the top of the pillars in the porch were of lily design, four cubits.
11_7_19.NLT: The capitals on the columns inside the entry room were shaped like water lilies, and they were six feet tall.
11_7_19.WEB: The capitals that were on the top of the pillars in the porch were of lily work, four cubits.
11_7_19.CSB17: And the capitals on top of the pillars in the portico were shaped like lilies, six feet high.
11_7_19.AMP: The capitals which were upon the top of the pillars in the porch were of lily work (design), four cubits.
11_7_19.NIV: The capitals on top of the pillars in the portico were in the shape of lilies, four cubits high.
11_7_19.NRSVCE: Now the capitals that were on the tops of the pillars in the vestibule were of lily-work, four cubits high.

11_7_20.NKJV: The capitals on the two pillars also had pomegranates above, by the convex surface which was next to the network; and there were two hundred such pomegranates in rows on each of the capitals all around.
11_7_20.KJV: And the chapiters upon the two pillars had pomegranates also above, over against the belly which was by the network: and the pomegranates were two hundred in rows round about upon the other chapiter.
11_7_20.ESV: The capitals were on the two pillars and also above the rounded projection which was beside the latticework. There were two hundred pomegranates in two rows all around, and so with the other capital.
11_7_20.NASB: [There were] capitals on the two pillars, even above [and] close to the rounded projection which was beside the network; and the pomegranates [numbered] two hundred in rows around both capitals.
11_7_20.NLT: The capitals on the two pillars had 200 pomegranates in two rows around them, beside the rounded surface next to the latticework.
11_7_20.WEB: There were capitals above also on the two pillars, close by the belly which was beside the network. There were two hundred pomegranates in rows around the other capital.
11_7_20.CSB17: The capitals on the two pillars were also immediately above the rounded surface next to the grating, and two hundred pomegranates were in rows encircling each capital.
11_7_20.AMP: The capitals were on the two pillars and also above and close to the rounded projection which was beside the network. There were two hundred pomegranates in rows around both capitals.
11_7_20.NIV: On the capitals of both pillars, above the bowl-shaped part next to the network, were the two hundred pomegranates in rows all around.
11_7_20.NRSVCE: The capitals were on the two pillars and also above the rounded projection that was beside the latticework; there were two hundred pomegranates in rows all around; and so with the other capital.

11_7_21.NKJV: Then he set up the pillars by the vestibule of the temple; he set up the pillar on the right and called its name Jachin, and he set up the pillar on the left and called its name Boaz.
11_7_21.KJV: And he set up the pillars in the porch of the temple: and he set up the right pillar, and called the name thereof Jachin: and he set up the left pillar, and called the name thereof Boaz. Jachin: that is, He shall establish Boaz: that is, In it is strength
11_7_21.ESV: He set up the pillars at the vestibule of the temple. He set up the pillar on the south and called its name Jachin, and he set up the pillar on the north and called its name Boaz.
11_7_21.NASB: Thus he set up the pillars at the porch of the nave; and he set up the right pillar and named it Jachin, and he set up the left pillar and named it Boaz.
11_7_21.NLT: Huram set the pillars at the entrance of the Temple, one toward the south and one toward the north. He named the one on the south Jakin, and the one on the north Boaz.
11_7_21.WEB: He set up the pillars at the porch of the temple. He set up the right pillar, and called its name Jachin; and he set up the left pillar, and called its name Boaz.
11_7_21.CSB17: He set up the pillars at the portico of the sanctuary: he set up the right pillar and named it Jachin; then he set up the left pillar and named it Boaz.
11_7_21.AMP: Hiram set up the pillars at the porch of the temple; he set up the right pillar and named it Jachin (may he establish), and he set up the left pillar and named it Boaz (in it is strength).
11_7_21.MSG: He set the pillars up in the entrance porch to The Temple; the pillar to the south he named Security (Jachin) and the pillar to the north Stability (Boaz).
11_7_21.NIV: He erected the pillars at the portico of the temple. The pillar to the south he named Jakin and the one to the north Boaz.
11_7_21.NRSVCE: He set up the pillars at the vestibule of the temple; he set up the pillar on the south and called it Jachin; and he set up the pillar on the north and called it Boaz.

11_7_22.NKJV: The tops of the pillars were in the shape of lilies. So the work of the pillars was finished.
11_7_22.KJV: And upon the top of the pillars was lily work: so was the work of the pillars finished.
11_7_22.ESV: And on the tops of the pillars was lily-work. Thus the work of the pillars was finished.
11_7_22.NASB: On the top of the pillars was lily design. So the work of the pillars was finished.
11_7_22.NLT: The capitals on the pillars were shaped like water lilies. And so the work on the pillars was finished.
11_7_22.WEB: On the top of the pillars was lily work: so the work of the pillars was finished.
11_7_22.CSB17: The tops of the pillars were shaped like lilies. Then the work of the pillars was completed.
11_7_22.AMP: On the tops of the pillars was lily work (design). So the work of the pillars was finished.
11_7_22.MSG: The capitals were in the shape of lilies.When the pillars were finished,
11_7_22.NIV: The capitals on top were in the shape of lilies. And so the work on the pillars was completed.
11_7_22.NRSVCE: On the tops of the pillars was lily-work. Thus the work of the pillars was finished.

11_7_23.NKJV: And he made the Sea of cast bronze, ten cubits from one brim to the other; it was completely round. Its height was five cubits, and a line of thirty cubits measured its circumference.
11_7_23.KJV: And he made a molten sea, ten cubits from the one brim to the other: it was round all about, and his height was five cubits: and a line of thirty cubits did compass it round about. from: Heb. from his brim to his brim
11_7_23.ESV: Then he made the sea of cast metal. It was round, ten cubits from brim to brim, and five cubits high, and a line of thirty cubits measured its circumference.
11_7_23.NASB: Now he made the sea of cast [metal] ten cubits from brim to brim, circular in form, and its height was five cubits, and thirty cubits in circumference.
11_7_23.NLT: Then Huram cast a great round basin, 15 feet across from rim to rim, called the Sea. It was 7 1⁄2 feet deep and about 45 feet in circumference.
11_7_23.WEB: He made the molten sea of ten cubits from brim to brim, round in shape. Its height was five cubits; and a line of thirty cubits encircled it.
11_7_23.CSB17: He made the cast metal basin, 15 feet from brim to brim, perfectly round. It was 7½ feet high and 45 feet in circumference.
11_7_23.AMP: Now he made the Sea (basin) of cast metal, ten cubits from brim to brim, circular in form, five cubits high and thirty cubits in circumference.
11_7_23.MSG: Hiram's next project was to make the Sea—an immense round basin of cast metal fifteen feet in diameter, seven and a half feet tall, and forty-five feet in circumference.
11_7_23.NIV: He made the Sea of cast metal, circular in shape, measuring ten cubits from rim to rim and five cubits high. It took a line of thirty cubits to measure around it.
11_7_23.NRSVCE: Then he made the molten sea; it was round, ten cubits from brim to brim, and five cubits high. A line of thirty cubits would encircle it completely.

11_7_24.NKJV: Below its brim were ornamental buds encircling it all around, ten to a cubit, all the way around the Sea. The ornamental buds were cast in two rows when it was cast.
11_7_24.KJV: And under the brim of it round about there were knops compassing it, ten in a cubit, compassing the sea round about: the knops were cast in two rows, when it was cast.
11_7_24.ESV: Under its brim were gourds, for ten cubits, compassing the sea all around. The gourds were in two rows, cast with it when it was cast.
11_7_24.NASB: Under its brim gourds went around encircling it ten to a cubit, completely surrounding the sea; the gourds were in two rows, cast with the rest.
11_7_24.NLT: It was encircled just below its rim by two rows of decorative gourds. There were about six gourds per foot all the way around, and they were cast as part of the basin.
11_7_24.WEB: Under its brim around there were buds which encircled it for ten cubits, encircling the sea. The buds were in two rows, cast when it was cast.
11_7_24.CSB17: Ornamental gourds encircled it below the brim, ten every half yard, completely encircling the basin. The gourds were cast in two rows when the basin was cast.
11_7_24.AMP: Under its brim were gourds encircling it ten to a cubit, completely surrounding the Sea; the gourds were in two rows, cast in one piece with it.
11_7_24.MSG: Just under the rim there were two bands of decorative gourds, ten gourds to each foot and a half. The gourds were cast in one piece with the Sea.
11_7_24.NIV: Below the rim, gourds encircled it—ten to a cubit. The gourds were cast in two rows in one piece with the Sea.
11_7_24.NRSVCE: Under its brim were panels all around it, each of ten cubits, surrounding the sea; there were two rows of panels, cast when it was cast.

11_7_25.NKJV: It stood on twelve oxen: three looking toward the north, three looking toward the west, three looking toward the south, and three looking toward the east; the Sea was set upon them, and all their back parts pointed inward.
11_7_25.KJV: It stood upon twelve oxen, three looking toward the north, and three looking toward the west, and three looking toward the south, and three looking toward the east: and the sea was set above upon them, and all their hinder parts were inward.
11_7_25.ESV: It stood on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east. The sea was set on them, and all their rear parts were inward.
11_7_25.NASB: It stood on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east; and the sea [was set] on top of them, and all their rear parts [turned] inward.
11_7_25.NLT: The Sea was placed on a base of twelve bronze oxen, all facing outward. Three faced north, three faced west, three faced south, and three faced east, and the Sea rested on them.
11_7_25.WEB: It stood on twelve oxen, three looking toward the north, and three looking toward the west, and three looking toward the south, and three looking toward the east; and the sea was set on them above, and all their hindquarters were inward.
11_7_25.CSB17: It stood on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east. The basin was on top of them and all their hindquarters were toward the center.
11_7_25.AMP: It stood on twelve oxen, three facing north, three west, three south, and three east; the Sea was set on top of them, and all their rear parts pointed inward.
11_7_25.MSG: The Sea was set on twelve bulls, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east; the bulls faced outward supporting the Sea on their hindquarters.
11_7_25.NIV: The Sea stood on twelve bulls, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south and three facing east. The Sea rested on top of them, and their hindquarters were toward the center.
11_7_25.NRSVCE: It stood on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east; the sea was set on them. The hindquarters of each were toward the inside.

11_7_26.NKJV: It was a handbreadth thick; and its brim was shaped like the brim of a cup, like a lily blossom. It contained two thousand baths.
11_7_26.KJV: And it was an hand breadth thick, and the brim thereof was wrought like the brim of a cup, with flowers of lilies: it contained two thousand baths.
11_7_26.ESV: Its thickness was a handbreadth, and its brim was made like the brim of a cup, like the flower of a lily. It held two thousand baths.
11_7_26.NASB: It was a handbreadth thick, and its brim was made like the brim of a cup, [as] a lily blossom; it could hold two thousand baths.
11_7_26.NLT: The walls of the Sea were about three inches thick, and its rim flared out like a cup and resembled a water lily blossom. It could hold about 11,000 gallons of water.
11_7_26.WEB: It was a hand width thick. Its brim was worked like the brim of a cup, like the flower of a lily. It held two thousand baths.
11_7_26.CSB17: The basin was three inches thick, and its rim was fashioned like the brim of a cup or of a lily blossom. It held eleven thousand gallons.
11_7_26.AMP: It was a hand width thick, and its brim was made like the brim of a cup, like a lily blossom. It held two thousand baths.
11_7_26.MSG: The Sea was three inches thick and flared at the rim like a cup, or like a lily. It held about 11,500 gallons.
11_7_26.NIV: It was a handbreadth in thickness, and its rim was like the rim of a cup, like a lily blossom. It held two thousand baths.
11_7_26.NRSVCE: Its thickness was a handbreadth; its brim was made like the brim of a cup, like the flower of a lily; it held two thousand baths.

11_7_27.NKJV: He also made ten carts of bronze; four cubits was the length of each cart, four cubits its width, and three cubits its height.
11_7_27.KJV: And he made ten bases of brass; four cubits was the length of one base, and four cubits the breadth thereof, and three cubits the height of it.
11_7_27.ESV: He also made the ten stands of bronze. Each stand was four cubits long, four cubits wide, and three cubits high.
11_7_27.NASB: Then he made the ten stands of bronze; the length of each stand was four cubits and its width four cubits and its height three cubits.
11_7_27.NLT: Huram also made ten bronze water carts, each 6 feet long, 6 feet wide, and 4 1⁄2 feet tall.
11_7_27.WEB: He made the ten bases of brass. The length of one base was four cubits, four cubits its width, and three cubits its height.
11_7_27.CSB17: Then he made ten bronze water carts. Each water cart was 6 feet long, 6 feet wide, and 4½ feet high.
11_7_27.AMP: Then Hiram made ten bronze stands [for smaller basins]; the length of each stand was four cubits, its width was four cubits and its height was three cubits.
11_7_27.MSG: Hiram also made ten washstands of bronze. Each was six feet square and four and a half feet tall.
11_7_27.NIV: He also made ten movable stands of bronze; each was four cubits long, four wide and three high.
11_7_27.NRSVCE: He also made the ten stands of bronze; each stand was four cubits long, four cubits wide, and three cubits high.

11_7_28.NKJV: And this was the design of the carts: They had panels, and the panels were between frames;
11_7_28.KJV: And the work of the bases was on this manner: they had borders, and the borders were between the ledges:
11_7_28.ESV: This was the construction of the stands: they had panels, and the panels were set in the frames,
11_7_28.NASB: This was the design of the stands: they had borders, even borders between the frames,
11_7_28.NLT: They were constructed with side panels braced with crossbars.
11_7_28.WEB: The work of the bases was like this: they had panels; and there were panels between the ledges;
11_7_28.CSB17: This was the design of the carts: They had frames; the frames were between the cross-pieces,
11_7_28.AMP: This was the design of the stands: they had borders between the frames.
11_7_28.MSG: They were made like this: Panels were fastened to the uprights.
11_7_28.NIV: This is how the stands were made: They had side panels attached to uprights.
11_7_28.NRSVCE: This was the construction of the stands: they had borders; the borders were within the frames;

11_7_29.NKJV: on the panels that were between the frames were lions, oxen, and cherubim. And on the frames was a pedestal on top. Below the lions and oxen were wreaths of plaited work.
11_7_29.KJV: And on the borders that were between the ledges were lions, oxen, and cherubims: and upon the ledges there was a base above: and beneath the lions and oxen were certain additions made of thin work.
11_7_29.ESV: and on the panels that were set in the frames were lions, oxen, and cherubim. On the frames, both above and below the lions and oxen, there were wreaths of beveled work.
